body:
MILESTONE 3: OnTick (main loop) reviewed. Good news first, then two real findings.

GOOD -- this part is genuinely well-built: market-open/trade-allowed checks, minimum-viable-ATR gate (avoids dead-session noise), a timeframe-change guard that forces clean recalibration rather than trading on stale weights, a deposit guard, an equity-floor circuit breaker (halts at max(30% of starting balance, 40% of all-time peak)), and a careful TIME-based (not tick-count-based) phased initialization that avoids tick-storm race conditions. When execution is suspended for low edge, it explicitly keeps managing existing positions (trailing/breakeven/exit) rather than abandoning them, and only blocks new entries. This is solid defensive engineering, no changes needed here.

FINDING 1 -- fabricated R-multiples can leak into the learning system: in the trade-history processing block, when SL distance can't be determined (even after an ATR-based fallback), the code falls back to ASSUMING rMultiple = +2.5 for a win / -1.0 for a loss rather than reflecting what actually happened (search 'fallback: assume standard RR'). That fabricated R-multiple feeds RecordTradeResult(), which is the same pipeline that produces the win-rate/RR statistics your position sizing (and my growth-rate recommendation) depends on. If this fallback path triggers more than rarely, it silently inflates the system's self-assessed edge in exactly the cases where data quality was worst. Worth knowing how often this path actually fires in practice -- if it's rare (broker always reports SL correctly) this is low priority; if it's common, it should either skip recording that trade rather than fabricate a number, or flag it as low-confidence data.

FINDING 2 -- a third regime-tracking path that doesn't sync back: DetectCurrentRegime() wraps the hysteresis classifier but can override its result with a correlation-model prediction when that model's historical accuracy for the predicted regime is >=65%. That override updates local bookkeeping (regime-DNA drift, regime-age tracking, transition logging) but never writes back to g_RegimeState.currentRegime -- the value everything else (ValidateEdge, ValidateRisk, position sizing) actually reads. So when the correlation model disagrees with and outperforms the rule-based classifier, that better prediction currently affects only diagnostics, not the actual trade decision. Might be intentional (correlation model as a confirmation-only signal) or might be an unfinished wire-up like the regimeStrategies[9-11] slots from milestone 2 -- flagging rather than assuming either way.

Two real, specific findings out of a very large main loop -- most of it holds up well under scrutiny. Continuing into OnInit next, then I'll consolidate everything found so far into a prioritized fix list before touching any code, per your process: identify first, then apply reinforcement, then hardening, then compile.
